text: Meet Stan. This morning, Stan had one thing to do.
scene: Stan slumped and melted into a green couch in a dim living room at night, a window behind showing a dark navy sky. Big black caps MEET STAN. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Go for a run. That is it.
scene: A sticky note on a fridge reading GO FOR A RUN with an empty checkbox, a pair of running shoes doodled beside it, simple flat kitchen. One job.

text: One thing and all day to do it.
scene: Stan standing in his flat living room looking up at a big wall clock showing a whole day ahead, running shoes waiting by the door. All day to do it. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: He scrolled instead.
scene: Stan sunk on the couch scrolling a glowing phone, little swirl icons drifting off it, flat room. He scrolled instead. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: He snacked. He told himself he would start in five minutes
scene: Stan on the couch eating from a snack bag, crumbs flying, a small clock ticking nearby, flat room. Snacks and five more minutes. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: and now it is dark out.
scene: The living room window now dark navy night with a moon, Stan still sunk on the couch in the gloom. Now it is dark out. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The run never happened and Stan is melted into the couch
scene: Stan fully flopped deep into the green couch, limbs splayed, the untouched running shoes still by the door. The run never happened. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: feeling like a complete failure.
scene: Stan on the couch with a heavy gray guilt cloud right over his head and a sad line mouth. Feeling like a failure. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You know Stan.
scene: Stan on the couch with a big red arrow pointing at him, big black caps YOU KNOW STAN. He is familiar. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You might be Stan right now.
scene: Stan on the couch and a big red arrow pointing out toward the viewer, big black caps MAYBE YOU. You might be Stan. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: So here's the twist for Stan and for you.
scene: Stan on the couch with a big glowing curved arrow swooping around him, big black caps THE TWIST. A turn is coming. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: That guilt he is feeling is the lie.
scene: The gray guilt cloud over Stan's head with a big red LIE stamped across the cloud. The guilt is the lie. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Not the laziness.
scene: A split frame: the guilt cloud stamped LIE on one side, the plain word LAZINESS unstamped on the other. The guilt lies, not the laziness.

text: Stan is not broken and he is not weak.
scene: Stan standing up straight, no longer slumped, a green check beside him, big black caps NOT BROKEN. Stan is fine. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: His brain is doing something it's spent a million years getting very, very good at.
scene: Stan's round head opened at the top to show a simple brain with little gears turning, big caps 1,000,000 YEARS. An ancient skilled brain. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And the reason he feels so guilty about it
scene: Stan with the gray guilt cloud and a red question mark, a faint dotted line trailing back into the past. Why so guilty? (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: traces back to a demon that a monk invented in the desert
scene: A simple flat desert with a tiny lone monk and a small red cartoon demon, a dotted line running forward toward Stan. Traces back to a desert demon. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: 1600 years ago.
scene: The desert monk and red demon under a huge sun, big black caps 1,600 YEARS AGO.

text: Everybody knows this feeling.
scene: Stan sitting up looking at a glowing floating TASK bubble in front of him, simple flat room. Everybody knows this feeling. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The task is right there.
scene: The glowing TASK bubble hovering right in front of Stan's reaching hand, a red arrow labeled THE TASK. It is right there. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You even want to want to do it.
scene: Stan reaching for the TASK bubble with a strained face and a small thought bubble of himself wanting to want it. You want to want to. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And your body just will not launch.
scene: Stan straining but frozen, his feet rooted to the floor like a rocket that will not lift off, red caps WON'T LAUNCH. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: We call it laziness.
scene: Stan slumped with a big black caps LAZINESS label slapped over him. We call it laziness. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And we treat it like a flaw in your character.
scene: Stan with a red FLAW stamp on his chest and a sad look. Treated like a character flaw. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: But stop and ask the real question.
scene: Stan with a giant red question mark over his head, big black caps THE REAL QUESTION. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Why would evolution build a creature that is bad at doing things?
scene: A simple evolution line of stick creatures, with a red X over one labeled BAD AT DOING THINGS. Would evolution build that?

text: It wouldn't. What it actually built is a creature that is brilliant
scene: The same evolution line proudly presenting a stick creature with a green check, label BRILLIANT AT SAVING ENERGY.

text: at not wasting energy.
scene: Stan sitting calmly with a full glowing battery icon beside him, big caps SAVE ENERGY. Not wasting energy. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And those are not the same thing
scene: Two labeled boxes side by side, BAD AT DOING THINGS and SAVING ENERGY, with a big not-equal sign between them.

text: even though they feel like it.
scene: The same two boxes but blurred so they look alike, caption FEELS THE SAME. They feel like the same thing.

text: Because your brain underneath everything else it does
scene: Stan's round head opened to show the simple inside of his brain, flat. Underneath everything. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: is an energy accountant.
scene: Inside Stan's head a tiny stick-figure accountant in glasses sits at a desk guarding a stack of gold energy coins, big caps ENERGY ACCOUNTANT. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: For almost all of human history food was scarce
scene: A simple flat scene of an empty plate and a bare cupboard, big caps FOOD WAS SCARCE.

text: and never guaranteed.
scene: The empty plate with a red question mark over it, simple flat. Never guaranteed.

text: Every calorie you burned was a calorie
scene: A single glowing gold calorie coin held carefully in a hand, big caps EVERY CALORIE COUNTS.

text: you might desperately need later.
scene: Caveman Stan in a fur tucking a gold calorie coin into a pouch for later, simple flat cave. Save it for later. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: When a predator showed up or the next meal didn't,
scene: Caveman Stan tense as a saber-tooth tiger lurks at the edge of the frame, a glowing calorie coin nearby, simple flat. Predator or no meal. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: so your brain evolved one ruthless rule.
scene: Inside Stan's head the accountant stamping a single big rule onto paper, big caps ONE RULE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Never spend effort you do not have to spend.
scene: A big bold sign reading NEVER SPEND EFFORT YOU DON'T HAVE TO, Stan reading it. The ruthless rule. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Scientists call it the law of least effort.
scene: A simple chalkboard with LAW OF LEAST EFFORT in big black caps, the accountant pointing at it.

text: And it runs so deep you do not even notice it.
scene: Stan walking along unaware while a hidden gear turns beneath his feet, label YOU DON'T NOTICE. Runs deep. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: When you walk across a room, your body
scene: Stan mid-stride walking across a simple flat room. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: is constantly making tiny adjustments
scene: Stan walking with tiny dotted adjustment arrows all over his body fine-tuning each step, big caps TINY ADJUSTMENTS. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: to use the least energy possible.
scene: Stan walking smoothly while an energy meter is sipped very slowly, big caps LEAST ENERGY. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You are, right down to the cell,
scene: A zoom on a single simple cell drawn as a circle with one tiny energy spark, big caps DOWN TO THE CELL.

text: tuned to do as little as you can get away with.
scene: Stan lounging happily doing the bare minimum, content face, big caps AS LITTLE AS POSSIBLE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: That is not a defect.
scene: The red DEFECT stamp from before, now crossed out with a big green line. Not a defect.

text: That is the setting that kept your ancestors alive.
scene: Caveman Stan alive and well beside a fire while faint faded figures behind him did not make it, big caps KEPT YOU ALIVE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: So when Stan cannot make himself lace up those shoes,
scene: Present-day Stan staring down at his running shoes, unable to make himself reach for them, simple flat room. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: that is not weakness talking.
scene: Stan and the running shoes with a red WEAKNESS label crossed out by a green line. Not weakness. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: That is his brain running a quiet calculation.
scene: Inside Stan's head the accountant scribbling on a notepad, big caps CALCULATING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The effort of the run on one side,
scene: A simple balance scale, the heavy left side holding a big running shoe labeled EFFORT, sinking down.

text: the reward on the other,
scene: The same balance scale, the light right side holding a small faint icon labeled REWARD, floating up.

text: and the reward being a little healthier
scene: The scale's reward side holds a tiny faint heart labeled HEALTHIER, very small.

text: in some far off future is fuzzy and far away.
scene: The faint little heart drawn far off in the distance, blurry, big caps FAR OFF FUTURE.

text: So the brain votes, not worth it, and pulls the plug.
scene: Inside Stan's head the accountant stamping a big red NOT WORTH IT and yanking a plug from the wall. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The exact instinct that once stopped Stan's ancestors
scene: Caveman Stan sitting still and calm by the fire instead of wandering off, big caps SAVED HIM. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: from wandering off and burning energy for no reason
scene: Caveman Stan refusing to wander into the dark, a red X over a path leading away, label NO POINTLESS BURNING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: is the one gluing him to the couch tonight.
scene: A dotted line connecting calm caveman Stan to present-day Stan glued to the couch, big caps SAME WIRING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The wiring is perfect.
scene: Stan's head opened showing neat tidy wiring with a green check, big caps PERFECT WIRING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: It just never got the memo that the danger is gone
scene: The brain wiring holding an old note while a crossed-out saber-tooth fades away, big caps DANGER GONE.

text: and the fridge is full.
scene: Stan on the couch beside a wide-open fridge stuffed full of food, big caps FRIDGE IS FULL. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Okay. So if doing nothing is this natural,
scene: Stan shrugging on the couch with a big question mark, caption SO NATURAL. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: this built in, why on earth does it feel like a sin?
scene: Stan on the couch with a red SIN stamp floating over him and a big question mark, big caps WHY A SIN? (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Because strangely, it once literally was one.
scene: An old book opening to reveal a red SIN stamp inside, caption IT ONCE WAS.

text: Rewind to the deserts of Egypt around the fourth century.
scene: A simple flat desert of sand dunes under a huge hot yellow sun with a tiny robed monk, big caps EGYPT, 4TH CENTURY.

text: Christian monks are living alone,
scene: A lone robed monk sitting cross-legged alone in the flat desert.

text: trying to focus on God all day,
scene: The monk looking up toward a glowing sun, hands together, big caps FOCUS ON GOD.

text: and they keep getting ambushed by a very specific enemy.
scene: The monk being ambushed by a sneaky red cartoon demon creeping up behind him, big caps A SPECIFIC ENEMY.

text: It hits in the heat of the afternoon,
scene: The desert at harsh high noon, the sun directly overhead beating down on the slumping monk, big caps THE AFTERNOON.

text: a heavy, restless, hollow feeling
scene: The monk slumped with a heavy hollow gray feeling around him, restless and empty.

text: where you cannot care about anything and cannot sit still.
scene: The monk with a blank empty face, fidgeting, unable to care or sit still, big caps CAN'T CARE.

text: They gave it a name, acedia,
scene: The red demon with a name tag, big black caps ACEDIA.

text: and they called it the noonday demon.
scene: The red demon hovering under the noon sun, big black caps THE NOONDAY DEMON.

text: A monk named Evagrius wrote it down
scene: A robed monk labeled EVAGRIUS writing on a scroll with a quill, simple flat.

text: as one of the eight great temptations of the human soul.
scene: A scroll listing eight little demon icons in a row, big black caps 8 TEMPTATIONS.

text: A couple of centuries later, the church tidied that list of eight down to seven.
scene: A church hand crossing out a list, the number 8 changed to 7, big caps 8 to 7.

text: Acedia got folded in and given a new name.
scene: The acedia demon being folded into a box with a fresh new name tag, big caps RENAMED.

text: One you already know, sloth, one of the seven deadly sins.
scene: A list of seven sins with one line circled and glowing red, big black caps SLOTH, a small DEADLY SIN stamp.

text: So sit with that for a second.
scene: A heavy red SIN stamp sitting alone, caption SIT WITH THAT.

text: The very first time anyone declared not feeling like it
scene: A speech bubble reading NOT FEELING LIKE IT with a red EVIL stamp slapped on it.

text: to be evil, it had nothing to do with work or chores.
scene: The NOT FEELING LIKE IT idea beside a crossed-out hammer and broom, big caps NOT ABOUT CHORES.

text: It was a spiritual demon that haunted monks at lunchtime.
scene: The red demon hovering over a monk eating lunch at a small table at noon, big caps LUNCHTIME DEMON.

text: For a long time, that is all it was, a religious idea.
scene: An old dusty religious book closed on a shelf, big caps JUST A RELIGIOUS IDEA.

text: Then came the part that actually planted the guilt
scene: A glowing red guilt seed being dropped down toward a chest, big caps PLANTED THE GUILT.

text: in your chest today.
scene: Present-day Stan with a small red guilt knot glowing in his chest, big caps TODAY. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: As workshops turned into factories,
scene: A small workshop hut morphing into a big smoking factory, big caps WORKSHOPS to FACTORIES.

text: a powerful new idea took hold.
scene: A glowing lightbulb appearing over the factory, big caps A NEW IDEA.

text: That hard work itself was holy and idle time was wasted time.
scene: A glowing halo over the word WORK and a trash bin swallowing the words IDLE TIME, big caps WORK = HOLY.

text: Stolen from God and very conveniently from your boss.
scene: Idle time being scooped away by a boss figure while a hand points up to the sky, big caps STOLEN.

text: Clocks went up on the walls, living to work
scene: A big clock being mounted on a factory wall with tiny workers below, big caps CLOCKS ON THE WALLS.

text: became a virtue you could brag about.
scene: A worker proudly flexing under the factory clock wearing a badge, big caps LIVING TO WORK.

text: And resting got quietly rebranded
scene: A cozy couch labeled REST getting a fresh new label slapped over it, big caps REBRANDED.

text: from a normal thing every human does into a moral failure.
scene: The same couch now stamped with a red MORAL FAILURE stamp, big caps NORMAL to FAILURE.

text: That judgment never left.
scene: The red MORAL FAILURE stamp stuck fast and never peeling off, big caps NEVER LEFT.

text: Every time you feel guilty for doing nothing,
scene: Present-day Stan on the couch with the gray guilt cloud back over him, big caps EVERY TIME. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: you are feeling the echo of a few hundred year old sales pitch.
scene: An old-timey salesman figure holding a sign reading LIVE TO WORK, big caps A 400-YEAR-OLD SALES PITCH.

text: So here's the quiet truth underneath all of it.
scene: Stan sitting calmly bathed in a soft warm glow, caption THE QUIET TRUTH. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: There is nothing wrong with you.
scene: Stan standing with a big calm green check, gentle big caps NOTHING WRONG WITH YOU. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: Two things are colliding inside you and neither one is a flaw.
scene: Stan in the center with two arrows pushing in from each side, neither stamped a flaw, big caps TWO THINGS COLLIDING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The first is an ancient, genuinely brilliant instinct
scene: On Stan's left a warm glowing caveman-instinct figure, big caps ANCIENT INSTINCT. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: to conserve energy.
scene: The caveman-instinct figure holding a glowing battery, big caps CONSERVE ENERGY.

text: The thing that carried humans through 100,000 years of scarcity.
scene: The caveman-instinct carrying tiny stick people along a long timeline, big caps 100,000 YEARS.

text: The second is a much newer idea that says
scene: On Stan's right a newer figure made of a monk plus a factory clock, big caps A NEWER IDEA. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: that instinct is shameful.
scene: The monk-and-clock figure aiming a red SHAMEFUL stamp at the instinct, big caps SHAMEFUL.

text: You are not lazy.
scene: Stan standing tall with a green check, big black caps NOT LAZY. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You are a finely tuned survival machine
scene: Stan drawn as a neat finely-tuned machine-stickman with smooth simple gears, big caps SURVIVAL MACHINE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: being graded by a rule book that was written
scene: The machine-Stan being stamped with a grade by a rulebook held in a boss's hand, big caps GRADED. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: to keep factories running.
scene: The rulebook held up, its cover showing a tiny factory, big caps MADE FOR FACTORIES.

text: Which means lazy was never really one thing.
scene: The word LAZY splitting apart into two different puzzle pieces, big caps NEVER ONE THING.

text: Sometimes it is your brain correctly telling you
scene: Puzzle piece one: the accountant pointing at a balance scale with a green check, big caps SOMETIMES RIGHT.

text: the reward is not worth the effort.
scene: A balance scale clearly showing a small reward outweighed by big effort, big caps NOT WORTH IT.

text: And honestly, that one is worth listening to.
scene: Stan nodding and listening to the little accountant, a green check, big caps WORTH LISTENING TO. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And sometimes it is a task that looks enormous
scene: Puzzle piece two: Stan facing a task drawn as a huge looming boulder, big caps THE TASK. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: to your energy saving brain,
scene: Inside Stan's head the accountant recoiling from the huge boulder task, big caps TOO BIG. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: but is actually small once you are moving
scene: The same boulder shrunk tiny once Stan takes a step and starts moving, big caps SMALL ONCE MOVING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: because the first step always costs the most
scene: A staircase where the first stair is enormous and the rest are tiny, big caps THE FIRST STEP COSTS THE MOST.

text: and your inner accountant charges you
scene: The accountant handing Stan one giant bill for the whole trip, big caps CHARGED UP FRONT. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: for the whole journey up front.
scene: Stan staring at the big upfront bill, big caps THE WHOLE JOURNEY. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: The fix was never to shame yourself harder.
scene: A sign reading SHAME YOURSELF HARDER crossed out with a big green line, big caps NOT THE FIX.

text: It is to shrink that first step
scene: The enormous first stair being chopped down into one tiny easy step, big caps SHRINK THE FIRST STEP.

text: until the accountant stops objecting.
scene: The accountant in Stan's head lowering his stamp and nodding, calm, big caps ACCOUNTANT STOPS OBJECTING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You are not fighting a weakness.
scene: Stan with a red WEAKNESS label crossed out, big caps NOT A WEAKNESS. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You are negotiating with a very old, very stubborn instinct.
scene: Stan calmly shaking hands with a stubborn old caveman-instinct figure, big caps NEGOTIATING. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: So the next time it is you on that couch,
scene: Stan back on the green couch at night, calm now, the running shoes still by the door, big caps NEXT TIME. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: the run not done, that little voice calling you lazy,
scene: Stan on the couch with a tiny voice bubble whispering LAZY, the run still not done. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: you will know what is really happening.
scene: Stan on the couch with a knowing calm face and a small glowing lightbulb, big caps YOU'LL KNOW. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: A million year old survival instinct
scene: On Stan's left a glowing caveman-instinct, big black caps MILLION-YEAR INSTINCT. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: just ran into a few hundred year old guilt trip
scene: On Stan's right a monk-and-clock guilt figure, big black caps FEW-HUNDRED-YEAR GUILT. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: and you like Stan got caught in the middle.
scene: Stan sitting calm in the middle as the two figures gently collide on either side, big caps CAUGHT IN THE MIDDLE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You were never broken.
scene: Stan standing relaxed with a big calm green check, big black caps NEVER BROKEN. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: You have just been running very, very old software.
scene: Stan with a thought bubble showing tiny old gears and a floppy disk, big black caps VERY OLD SOFTWARE. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)

text: And now, at least, you know who wrote it.
scene: Stan at peace on the couch looking knowingly at the little old gears, the tiny monk and caveman behind him, caption NOW YOU KNOW. (Stan is always the same plain crude stick figure: round white head, two dot eyes, a simple line mouth, thin line body and limbs, no hair.)
